title: polystichopsis macrostegia
intro: Polystichopsis macrostegia, also known as the Japanese holly fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Polystichopsis macrostegia are said to include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although the safety and efficacy of these preparations have not been extensively studied.
